intent: Waterfall chart: a running cumulative total drawn as floating bars. The X axis is the CATEGORY/PERIOD field — one bar per category, sort:null to keep input order. X is NEVER the numeric measure. Each bar spans from the previous cumulative total (_prev) up to the new total (_sum); a window sum over __FIELD__ builds the totals and bars are colored by increase/decrease. Substitute __FIELD__ with the numeric measure column and "category" with the category/period field (the axis of progression, e.g. month).

transform.calculate: if(datum.type === 'Strongly disagree',-2,0) + if(datum.type==='Disagree',-1,0) + if(datum.type =='Neither agree nor disagree',0,0) + if(datum.type ==='Agree',1,0) + if(datum.type ==='Strongly agree',2,0)
if(datum.type === 'Disagree' || datum.type === 'Strongly disagree', datum.percentage,0) + if(datum.type === 'Neither agree nor disagree', datum.percentage / 2,0)

intent: The Trellis display by Becker et al. helped establish small multiples as a “powerful mechanism for understanding interactions in studies of how a response depends on explanatory variables”. Here we reproduce a trellis of Barley yields from the 1930s, complete with main-effects ordering to facilitate comparison.

spec.name: trellis_barley
spec.description: The Trellis display by Becker et al. helped establish small multiples as a “powerful mechanism for understanding interactions in studies of how a response depends on explanatory variables”. Here we reproduce a trellis of Barley yields from the 1930s, complete with main-effects ordering to facilitate comparison.

intent: Heatmap of an aggregated value over two categorical/time axes (rect mark with aggregated color)
Heatmap with numeric text labels overlaid (layered rect + text sharing one aggregate transform, conditional text color)
Bump chart: ranking over time computed with a window rank transform (line with points, color per series)
Candlestick (OHLC) chart: layered rule (low–high) + bar (open–close) with an up/down conditional color
Streamgraph: stacked area with a centered baseline (stack:'center'), color per series
Ranged dot / dumbbell plot: a connecting line plus two colored points per category to show change between two states
Parallel coordinates: fold several numeric fields into key/value and draw one line per record (detail groups each line)
Line with a confidence-interval band (errorband + mean line layered)
Scatter plot with a rolling-mean overlay (windowed moving average line over points)
Normalized (100%) stacked bar chart using stack:'normalize'
Strip plot with random jitter: a calculate transform produces a random offset bound to yOffset to reduce overplotting
Violin plot: a mirrored density curve per group (density transform + horizontal area with x stack:'center'), one column facet per group. Where __FIELD__ appears, substitute a real numeric column

Faceted violin (density area per group) with raw data points overlaid as a centered jittered strip. KEY STRUCTURE: to facet a LAYERED chart you MUST use the 'facet' operator wrapping the layer spec ({facet, spec:{layer:[...]}}) — putting row/column in a layered spec's shared encoding silently fails to facet (all groups collapse into one cell). The density transform is scoped INSIDE the violin layer so the points are not aggregated. Points share the violin's density x-axis and are centered with x:{value:<half of width>} and jittered per-point via mark.xOffset {expr:'(random()-0.5)*N'}. Where __FIELD__ appears, substitute a real numeric column; 'group' is the grouping category
Line chart with an arrow annotation — a rule segment for the shaft, a rotated filled triangle point as the arrowhead, and a text label
